university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The couse is focused on basic economic categories and their interdependence. Student becomes familiar with basic models in the field of economics.
course_content: 1.Introduction into economics (allowance 1/0) a. Economics -- science about human dealings (social science) b. Function of prices c. Economic (business) cycle 2.Consumer and producer behaviour (allowance 2/0) a. Demand and utility b. Price elasticity of demand c. Supply and costs d. Price elasticity of supply 3.Market equilibrium (allowance 1/0) a. Efficiency of market equilibrium b. Change of equilibrium c. State price interventions 4.International trade (allowance 2/0) a. Absolute advantages b. Comparative advantages c. Import tariff 5.Perfect and imperfect markets (allowance 3/0) a. Perfect markets b. Monopolist competition c. Oligopoly d. Monopoly 6.Market of producer factors (allowance 2/0) a. Demand for a producer factor b. Supply of a producer factor c. Labour market d. Capital market e. Unemployment 7.Ownership and externalities (allowance 2/0) a. Private and public ownership b. Ownership vs. management c. Positive and negative externalities 8.Domestic product (allowance 1/0) a. Gross and net domestic product b. Nominal and real domestic product c. Domestic product as an added value d. Aggregate expenses and incomes 9.Consumption and investments (allowance 1/0) a. Consumption function b. Planned investments and equilibrium GDP c. Income effect of investments 10.Money and banking system (allowance 1/0) a. Money balances as liquid assets b. Demand for money balances c. Banks and money creation d. Money stock e. Money market equilibrium 11.Aggregate demand and supply (allowance 1/0) a. Aggregate demand b. Aggregate supply c. Potential product 12.Economic (business) cycle and economic growth (allowance 1/0) a. Stages of economic cycle b. Financial crisis c. Economic growth 13.Inflation (allowance 1/0) a. Inflation and redistribution b. Demand-pull inflation c. Supply-shock inflation d. Phillips curve 14.Exchange rate and balance of payments (allowance 3/0) a. Interest rate parity b. Purchasing power parity c. Balance of payments 15.Public finances (allowance 2/0) a. Public budgets and state budget b. Taxes and Laffer curve c. Budgetary deficits and state debt 16.Monetary policy (allowance 2/0) a. Goals and instruments of monetary policy b. Compatibility of monetary policy goals c. Inflation behaviour
learning_outcomes: - identify the scope of economic research - define and explain basic economic categories and causalities - characterize behavior of both consumer as well as firm on micro level - examine economic policy of government and central bank on macro level
assessment_methods: Term paper: 40 % Final test: 60 %
literature: Basic: Holman, R. (2005). Ekonomie. C.H. Beck. Recommended: Macáková, L. (2010). Mikroekonomie : základní kurs. Melandrium. Pavelka, T. (2007). Makroekonomie : základní kurz. Melandrium. Pošta, V., & Sirůček, P. (2008). Makroekonomie : základní kurz : cvičebnice. Melandrium. Macáková, L., Nečadová, M., & Sirůček, P. (2003). Mikroekonomická teorie I. : cvičebnice. Melandrium.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: Accounting as an information tool for the decision-making of an entity’s owners, managers, investors in capital markets, creditors and other users of financial statements. Fundamental elements of financial statements and their content. Impacts of transactions and events on financial position and performance. European and worldwide regulation and harmonisation of accounting.
course_content: 1.Introduction to accounting: the nature, principles of financial accounting, characteristics of accounting. Objectives of financial accounting, scope of financial reporting, users of financial statements. Audit. Publishing of financial statements. Annual Report. (allowance 0/2) a. Objectives of financial accounting, scope of financial reporting b. Users of financial statements c. Audit. Publishing of financial statements. Annual Report 2.Accounting categories and definitions, characteristics of accounting elements, accounting cycle, standardization of financial reporting. (allowance 0/2) a. Elements of accounting method: balance sheet equation, nature of accounts b. Documentation, controls, measurement; transaction analysis c. Trial balance 3.Income statement accounts (allowance 0/2) a. Expenditures and receipts b. Expense and revenue c. Classification of expenses by nature d. Classification of expenses by function 4.Business models: a trade company vs. a manufacturer. (allowance 0/2) 5.Conceptual framework. Features of world harmonization process (allowance 0/2) a. EU Directives b. US GAAP c. IFRS 6.Non-current assets (allowance 0/2) a. Definition, classification b. Measurement, depreciation and amortisation 7.Inventory (allowance 0/2) a. Definition, classification b. Classification 8.Investments (allowance 0/2) a. Definition, classification b. Presentation c. Measurement d. Transactions 9.Receivables and revenues (allowance 0/2) a. Recognition principle b. Measurement c. Accruals assets d. Contingent assets 10.Liabilities and provisions (allowance 0/2) a. Classification b. Measurement c. Accruals and deferrals, contingent liabilities 11.Equity (allowance 0/2) a. Classification b. Features of different types of companies c. Capital contribution and distribution d. Transactions e. Statements of Changes in Equity f. Statement of Comprehensive Income 12.Cash flow statement (allowance 0/2) a. Notes to financial statements 13.Decision-usefulness of accounting data and its limits. Financial statements analysis. (allowance 0/2)
learning_outcomes: - understand the content of financial statements for external users; - define and explain the nature of fundamental elements of financial statements; - record the basic economic transactions on the face of financial statements; - master the accounting cycle, including double-entry; - prepare an elementary set of financial statements; - describe the main features of accounting regulation and harmonisation both in national and international context.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Term paper: 40 % Final test: 54 %
literature: Basic: Janhuba, M., Míková, M., Roubíčková, J., & Zelenka, V. (2016). Finanční účetnictví : obecné otázky. C.H. Beck. Janhuba, M. (2010). Teorie účetnictví : (výběr z problematiky). Oeconomica. Bokšová, J., Dvořáková, D., Pelák, J., Procházka, D., Roubíčková, J., Skálová, J., Svoboda, M., Vašek, L., Zelenková, M., & Černý, V. (2015). Účetnictví I. - sbírka příkladů a úloh k procvičení. Oeconomica. Recommended: Pelák, J. (2009). Účetnictví v příkladech : repetitorium k základům účetnictví. Oeconomica. DVOŘÁKOVÁ, Dana. Základy účetnictví. Praha: Wolters Kluwer Česká republika, 2010. ISBN 978-80-7357-544-1.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of the course is that students get to know to the issue of marketing and commercial communications and with particular tools of commercial communications (advertisement, direct marketing, sales promotion, public relations, sponsoring and on-line communications) comprehensively. Usage of communication mix within integrated marketing communications, with respect to corporate social responsibility, is stressed. Apart from that, students also learn how to apply communication theory in advertisement. The issue of media is explained in the context of medial strategy and media mix for communication campaign.
course_content: 1.Marketing and commercial communication (allowance 2/0) a. Marketing communication, substance, characteristics b. Division of marketing communications (ATL and BTL, trends). Integrated marketing communications (IMC). c. c. Forms of commercial communications (substance, characteristics of different forms).c. Forms of commercial communications (substance, characteristics of different forms).Forms of commercial communications (substance, characteristics of different forms). 2.Goals of commercial communications (allowance 2/0) a. Business goal vs. marketing goal vs. goal of commercial communications (relations). b. Possibilities of measuring efficiency of commercial communications in relation to their goal. c. Researches in the field of commercial communications. 3.Target groups in commercial communications (allowance 2/0) a. Marketing segmentation, segments and their definition. b. Target groups in commercial communications. c. Target groups in advertising and media. 4.Message in commercial communications (allowance 2/0) a. Message and appeals in commercial communications. b. Types of appeals used in commercial communications. c. Risks of some statements in advertising. 5.Media in commercial communications (allowance 2/0) a. Medium and its definition, types of media. b. Characteristics of selected media. c. Media mix and its creation, media strategy. 6.Media indicators used in commercial communications. (allowance 2/0) a. Media indicators used in commercial communications. b. Correlation between media indicators. c. Examples of selected media indicators. Media researches. 7.Advertising means and their application (allowance 2/0) a. Advertising mean and its substance. b. Types of advertising means. c. Advertisement, its creation, types, suitable applications. 8.Time schedule and budget in communication campaign (allowance 2/0) a. Time schedule in advertising campaign. b. Models used in TV spots setting. c. Budget in communication campaign. 9.Client and creative brief (allowance 0/0) a. Client brief -- content, creation principles. b. Creative brief -- content, creation principles. c. Necessary and forbidden elements in advertising as part of Client brief. 10.Law and ethics in commercial communications (allowance 2/0) a. Law in commercial communications (private, public). b. Ethics in commercial communications (self-regulation). c. Ethics code RPR. 11.Advertising chain and its individual links (allowance 2/0) a. Clients (submitters of advertising), professional association ČSZV. b. Role of media agency and media representation. c. Advertising agency and media as part of advertising chain. 12.Communication agency and its individual departments (allowance 2/0) a. Management of a communication agency, traffic role. b. Characteristics of individual departments of a communication agency. c. Communication agency tender from an advertising submitter. 13.Competition for advertising efficiency EFFIE (allowance 2/0) a. World advertising competition (World advertising festival in Cannes, Clio). b. Competition EFFIE (history, substance, evaluation criteria, competition categories). c. Presentation of competition EFFIE (DVD show with comments).
learning_outcomes: -understand the fundamentals of particular forms of marketing and commercial communications, -propose communication mix and media mix for a particular communication campaign, -understand communicational basics of particular advertisement campaign and assess its effectiveness -prepare theses of a project of communication campaign with a given topic -judge communication campaign from the point of view of recommended principles and targets set
assessment_methods: Term paper: 55 % Final test: 45 %
literature: Basic: VYSEKALOVÁ, Jitka; MIKEŠ, Jiří. Reklama. Jak dělat reklamu. 4. vyd. Praha: Grada Publishing, a.s, 2018. 232 p. ISBN 978-80-247-5865-7. JESENSKÝ, Daniel; KARLÍČEK, Miroslav; KRÁL, Petr; POUCHA, Tomáš; STŘÍTESKÝ, Václav; ZAMAZALOVÁ, Marcela. Marketingová komunikace : jak komunikovat na našem trhu. Praha: Grada Publishing, 2016. ISBN 978-80-247-5769-8. BÁRTA, Vladimír; POSTLER, Milan. Retail marketing : (přepracované vydání). Praha: Press21, 2020. ISBN 978-80-907529-1-7. KRUPKA, Jaroslav. Česká reklama : od pana Vajíčka po falešné soby. Brno: BizBooks, 2012. ISBN 978-80-265-0046-9.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: A good knowledge of the office pack is useful for students both for elaboration of seminar papers, bachelor and master thesis and in everyday business. This course focuses on advanced functions of office software - data analysis spread sheets, programmes for text elaboration, presentation and for creating, recording, categorizing and searching information. With respect to the equipment of classrooms at the University of Economics in Prague, Microsoft Excel, Microsoft Word, Microsoft PowerPoint, Microsoft OneNote and Adobe Acrobat are used in this course. Successful completion of this course can be a good starting point for passing tests in 3 modules (3, 4, 6) of the international certificate ECDL. ECDL testing is available (a fee is charged) at many testing centres in the Czech Republic. In general, knowledge of 7 modules is tested (1. Concepts of ICT, 2. Using the computer and managing files, 3. Word processing, 4. Spread sheets, 5. Using databases, 6. Graphic facilities of PC and ways of electronic presentation, 7. Services of information network). The ECDL certificate is common in the EU countries, where it is widely used and required especially in the state administration. Known as ICDL, it is also popular outside Europe.
course_content: 1.Data analysis (allowance 0/12) a. Office programmes for data analysis. b. Introduction - Microsoft Office Excel. c. Possibilities of cells formatting, absolute and relative addressing and files editing. d. Inserting graphs, functions and other objects. e. Possibilities of work and display of files. f. Data work, contingence tables. g. Use of macros and the Visual Basic language. 2.Text editingoffice programmes for text editing (allowance 0/6) a. Introduction - Microsoft Office Word. b. Styles and patterns. c. Tables. d. Images, diagrams and other objects in "word" documents. e. Lists, resumé and contents. f. Various ways of document view and work with documents. g. Collective correspondence. h. Cooperation with other applications. i. Application setting. j. File conversion into PDF and various setting possibilities (bookmarks, security, document creation from various sources). k. Further document elaboration in the PDF format (adding, cutting off pages, heading, numbers of pages, work with images, hypertext references). l. Forms in PDF. 3.Presentation (allowance 0/8) a. Office programmes used for presentations. b. Introduction - Microsoft Office PowerPoint. c. Inserting images, slides, work with patterns. d. Presentation editing by adding slide animations. e. Other options during projection.
learning_outcomes: - use advanced functions of office software effectively, - analyse data in the spread sheet application Microsoft Excel, - prepare well-arranged text documents in Microsoft Word, - prepare impressive presentations in Microsoft PowerPoint, - process information in Microsoft OneNote. The students will be able to: - prepare individual presentation on IT topic within seminar work, - defence their own project (both at colloquium and during defence).
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Term paper: 18 % Mid-term test(s): 38 % Final test: 26 % Home works: 12 %
literature: Basic: Kubálková, M., Kubálek, T., & Topolová, I. (2013). Tabulkový program Microsoft Excel 2013. Nakladatelství Oeconomica. Topolová, I., Kubálek, T., & Kubálková, M. (2013). Textový procesor Microsoft Word 2013. Nakladatelství Oeconomica. Kubálek, T., & Kubálková, M. (2009). Prezentační program Microsoft Office PowerPoint : verze 2007 CZ. Oeconomica. Kubálková, M., & Kubálek, T. (2014). Microsoft OneNote 2013. Program na uchovávání a sdílení informací (2nd ed.). Nakladatelství Oeconomica. Recommended: KUBÁLEK, Tomáš; ANDRES, Pavel. Informatika. Tabulkový program Microsoft Excel. 1. vyd. Praha: České vysoké učení technické v Praze, Masarykův ústav vyšších studií, 2023. 206 s.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: With the growing integration of Czech enterprises into international cooperation, the need to know management techniques common in the USA and the EU is becoming more important. Project management and project style of company management are some of these techniques. The aim of this course is to teach students how to plan, control and manage projects with the use of a computer. A demonstration of software for project management and diagram creation is a part of the course. Thanks to the DreamSpark programme, students will have the possibility to use Microsoft Project and Microsoft Visio both during the course as well as during their individual study.
course_content: 1.Project management (allowance 0/14) a. Fundamentals of project management theory. b. Introduction of Microsoft Office Project, project foundation. c. Calendars, their types, calendar administration. d. Objectives (tasks), their types, outlines, repeated task, codes of WBS outline, grouping, self outline, ranging and filtering. e. Resources, their types, resources assignment to tasks, change of duration, units of assignment, work, planning driven by effort, overloading by resources, delay of assignment, overtime work. f. Costs, their types, task costs, resource costs. g. Project process monitoring, types of monitoring, directive plan, overtime work, over-planning of incomplete work. h. Project display, its types, table and graph formats, form display, calendar display, network diagram, diagram of resources, self display. i. Display print. Use and creation of compilations. j. Project patterns. Organizer of project objects. Project publishing on web sites. Work on more projects. k. Further tools for project work: macros, arrays, graphic indicators, EVA and PERT analysis. 2.Scheme creation (allowance 0/10) a. Use of scheme in business processes analysis, project planning, display of thinking processes, graphic illustration of organization scheme, illustration of network infrastructures, plans of space layouts and equipment, software systems and database structures. b. Introduction of Microsoft Office Visio programme. c. Layers, styles, fixtures and points, flowlines, colour tables, use of figures. d. Drawing foundation, use of grab points, work with figures and their formatting. Drawing and figure editing. e. Work with group, flowlines and text. f. Print. g. Export and import into database systems and other programmes. h. Examples of scheme creation: diagram of development, map of business surroundings, table and graph of turnover development, project plan, calendar, time axis, organization scheme of a company, marketing graphs and diagrams, call system, building(house) with equipment. 3.Recording, screening and seeking for information (allowance 0/2) a. The importance of recording and structuring personal remarks (during task solution) and project realization. b. Introduction of Microsoft Office OneNote programme. c. Forms of remark recording (hand writing, keyboard writing, sound, video, remarks from PDA). d. Hierarchy of One Note agenda: components, divisions, pages, subpages, sets of formulas. e. Remarks organization: containers, bullets, lists, outlines, keyboard shortcuts. f. Cooperation with other programmes. g. Work with remarks: searching, signs. h. Remarks sharing.
learning_outcomes: - plan, control and manage projects with the use of the program Microsoft Project, - create multiple diagrams or plans in Microsoft Visio. Students will also be able to - propose an individual solution of their own project within seminar work, - defence their own project (both at colloquium and during defence).
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Term paper: 18 % Mid-term test(s): 38 % Final test: 26 % Home works: 12 %
literature: Basic: Kubálek, T., & Kubálková, M. (2015). Program pro řízení projektů Microsoft Project 2013 (1st ed.). Nakladatelství Oeconomica. Kubálek, T., & Kubálková, M. (2015). Program pro tvorbu diagramů Microsoft Visio 2013 (1st ed.). Nakladatelství Oeconomica.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: Students will get to know the possibilities of database systems, which are a part of office packs. Students will learn how to work with the database system Microsoft Access with the aim to use it in their bachelor and master thesis and in their future occupation. Thanks to the MSDN AA programme students will have the possibility to use the MS Access programme free of charge. Passing this course can be a good starting point to pass the test of module No. 5 of the international certificate ECDL. The ECDL certificate is common in the EU countries, where it is widely used and required especially in the state administration. Known as ICDL, it is also popular outside Europe.
course_content: 1.Basic database notions. (allowance 0/0) 2.Introduction of the Microsoft Office Access programme. (allowance 0/0) 3.Database constitution. (allowance 0/0) 4.Relation between tables in a database and ensuring reference integrity. (allowance 0/0) 5.Array specification and table characters. (allowance 0/0) 6.Enquiry creation. (allowance 0/0) 7.Creation of form for data inserting and print sets for their presentation. (allowance 0/0) 8.Creation of macros and fundamentals of Visual Basic. (allowance 0/0) 9.Cooperation of Access with other programmes, data export and import. (allowance 0/0) 10.Data publishing on web sites. (allowance 0/0)
learning_outcomes: work with the database system Microsoft Access, especially: - to define a database and relations between database tables; - to design queries; - to prepare forms and reports; - to use Visual Basic to adjust functions. Students will also be able to: - propose individual solution of an own database within seminar work - defence their own project (both at colloquium and during defence).
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Term paper: 18 % Mid-term test(s): 32 % Final test: 32 % Home works: 12 %
literature: Basic: Kubálek, T., & Kubálková, M. (2016). Databázový systém Microsoft Access 2016 (1st ed.). Nakladatelství Oeconomica. Kubálek, T. (2022). Manažerská informatika. Interaktivní analýza a vizualizace dat Microsoft Power BI (1st ed.). České vysoké učení technické v Praze, Masarykův ústav vyšších studií. https://horec-my.sharepoint.com/personal/kubalek_horec_onmicrosoft_com/Documents/MIN/1%20prezen%c4%8dn%c3%ad%20v%c3%bduka/MIN2/3%20Power%20BI/1%20u%c4%8debn%c3%ad%20text/PowerBI.pdf?ga=1

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: Team collaboration software tools are playing increasingly important role in every manager´s work. Cloud technologies are one of them. The aim of this course is to teach the students to work with a system of unified communications Office 365, especially to create team sites (intranet). In addition, students learn about customer relationship management (CRM) in sales, marketing and services. Thanks to support the company Microsoft provides to schools, the students can through the web interface use Office 365 (SharePoint, OneDrive for Business, Forms, Sway, Power Apps, Stream).
course_content: 1.The Basics of Office 365 (allowance 0/4) a. Introduction to the subject. b. Office 365 - principles and plans. c. OneDrive for Business - shared documents storage. d. Forms - creation of surveys. e. Record of meetings through Teams. 2.SharePoint - team collaboration with the focus on sharing information through team websites (allowance 0/12) a. Type of sites. b. Create of site. c. Libraries. d. Lists. e. Web parts. f. Export to other applications. g. User groups and permissions. h. Library and list properties. i. Site settings. j. G Suite - differences and benefits over Office 365. 3.Other Office 365 applications (allowance 0/10) a. Power BI - data analysis and visualization. b. Administration of Office 365. c. Groups of Office 365. d. Teams - center for team cooperation. e. Pulse - voting applications. f. Stream - the internal video portal of the company. g. Power Automate - flow in different applications. h. Power Apps - programming of mobile applications. i. Delve - searching for information in the intranet. j. MyAnalytics - analysis of working time utilization. k. Bookings - reservations in small and medium-sized businesses. l. StafHubb - shift planning. m. Planner and To-Do - task planning and tracking. n. Yammer - corporate social network.
learning_outcomes: - share information through team sites using the program Microsoft SharePoint, - create a website using Wix. - propose their own solution of team web within seminar paper, - defence their own project (both within colloquium and within defence).
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Term paper: 18 % Presentation or defence: 0 % Mid-term test(s): 32 % Final test: 32 % Home works: 12 %
literature: Basic: Kubálek, T. (2022). Manažerská informatika. Nástroj týmové spolupráce Microsoft Teams (1st ed.). (2022). Manažerská informatika. Interaktivní analýza a vizualizace dat Microsoft Power BI (1st ed.). university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The course is focused on the basics of company marketing management introducing useful terms, concepts and tools.
course_content: 1.Marketing and strategic marketing planning (allowance 4/0) a. Marketing, marketing vs production philosophy b. Marketing strategic planning, analysis of the interaction between internal and external environment of the business entity 2.Market segmentation (allowance 4/0) a. Categories of segmentation b. Segment and its definition c. Types of marketing segmentation 3.Branding and Positioning (allowance 4/0) a. Product and brand (substance, differences) b. Brands typology, principles of creating a successful brand c. Branding strategy 4.Marketing mix and its application in marketing management (allowance 4/0) a. Marketing product concept b. Product life cycle c. Product mix management 5.Marketing pricing methods (allowance 4/0) a. Cost-oriented pricing methods b. Required price c. Price based on perceived value d. Price based on price competition e. Psychological price 6.Distribution channels (allowance 4/0) a. Trade and its role in the supply chain b. Distribution channel strategy c. Forms of sales and their application 7.Communication policy (allowance 2/0) a. Marketing communication and message b. Communication mix
learning_outcomes: - understand the basics of marketing management and its relationship with the company overall strategy, - use the segmentation process and find the fit of the segment and the product, - characterize particular tools of marketing management and use proper models od 4P, - explore the influence of particular stakeholders on company management, - draft the company marketing plan.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Term paper: 34 % Final test: 60 %
literature: Basic: Karlíček, M. (2018). Základy marketingu. Grada Publishing, a.s. Recommended: Armstrong, G., & Kotler, P. (2004). Marketing. Grada. KOTLER, Philip; NOVÝ, Vladimír. Moderní marketing : 4. evropské vydání. Praha: Grada, 2007. ISBN 978-80-247-1545-2. Keller, K. L., & Kotler, P. (2007). Marketing management. Grada. Armstrong, G., & Kotler, P. (2012). Principles of marketing. Pearson. KOTLER, Philip. 10 smrtelných marketingových hříchů : jak je rozpoznat a nespáchat. Praha: Grada, 2005. ISBN 80-247-0969-4. Armstrong, G., Kotler, P., & Opresnik, M. O. (2021). Principles of marketing. Pearson.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The class aims at understanding current economic situation in the world as well as the relations between different actors of the global economy, which influence business environment in the Czech Republic and in Europe. In respect to globalization of the world economy, it should identify the main challenges of economic development in the European Union as well as in the Czech Republic. Analysis of economic crises, their consequences and reaction of the economic policy is another part of the course.
course_content: 1.Globalization of the world economy, basic characteristics and main trends of economic development (allowance 9/0) a. Economic growth; formal and informal institutions b. Effects of globalization on differentiation of living standards, flows and prices of goods. Potential for development of the international economic environment. c. Economic role of states, regional integrations and international organizations in the current world economy, basic stages of the world economy development d. The current world economic situation, causes and consequences of economic crises and their solutions 2.World economy centres and their economic environment (allowance 9/0) a. Economic policy of the state, competitiveness and its measurement, basic prerequisites for sustainable development of economic activity b. Europe -- postwar economic integration (EU, ESVO), economic systems and policies of the European countries, competitiveness of the European countries, transformation and association process of the central and eastern European countries, economic integration of the whole European region c. North America -- the USA as a dominant world economy centre, characteristics of its economic politics and international relations, NAFTA/USMCA, financial crisis and deficits of the USA d. BRIC and N11 countries -- the changing role of Japan, Asian newly industrialized countries, India and China as future economic powers; Russia, central Asia and Near East; Brazil and Latin America 3.European Union and its impact on the economic environment in Europe and the Czech Republic (allowance 8/0) a. Basic trends of the European integration process development b. Functioning of the European Union, institutions, budget and EU politics c. Internal market, monetary union and coordination of the EU economic policies d. Current situation and future challenges of the European Union
learning_outcomes: - understand basic trends of the current world economy including globalization, competitiveness and economic integration, - know the characteristics of world economy centres and understand differences in their economic systems and current development, - understand the functioning of the European Union and assess its impact on business environment in Europe and in the Czech Republic, - understand the significance of economic institutions and their influence on the business environment and economic growth, - understand the causes of economic crises and their repercussions for the business environment and economic policy, - apply knowledge of the global business environment on their economic or business activities.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 10 % Final test: 60 % Home assignments: 30 %
literature: Basic: HNÁT, Pavel. Politická ekonomie globálních nerovnováh. 1. vyd. Praha: Nakladatelství Oeconomica, 2014. 232 p. ISBN 978-80-245-2023-0. Baldwin, R. E., & Wyplosz, C. (2013). Ekonomie evropské integrace. Grada Publishing. Recommended: DICKEN, Peter. Global shift : mapping the changing contours of the world economy. New York: Guilford Press, 2015. ISBN 978-1-4625-1955-2. ACEMOGLU, Daron; ROBINSON, James A. Proč státy selhávají : kořeny moci, prosperity a chudoby. Praha: Argo, 2015. ISBN 978-80-257-1305-1. ALIBER, Robert Z.; KINDLEBERGER, Charles Poor. Manias, panics and crashes : a history of financial crises. Basingstoke: Palgrave Macmillan, 2011. ISBN 978-0-230-36535-3.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of the course is that students get familiar with business transactions that cross national boundaries. The course includes international trade in goods as well as bank, transport, insurance, marketing and related services both in the EU and in the third countries. Business transactions realized by both private and public sector are mentioned, with special emphasis on transactions of multinational corporations.
course_content: 1.Channels of distribution, definitions, types, factors of selection. Processing international business operations. (allowance 0/0) 2.Subject of international trade. (allowance 0/0) 3.Contracts for the international sale of goods. Definition, conclusion and forms of sales contracts, their contents. Fulfilment of the contract, documents used in international trade, transfer of data. (allowance 0/0) 4.Terms of delivery. Definitions and functions, Incoterms 2000. The relation of terms of delivery to other conditions of the contract. (allowance 0/0) 5.Price in international trade, criteria for price selection, price calculation, price and risk assessment of the transaction, level risk of transaction. Dumping, antidumping procedures, measures used against dumping. Essential price strategies. (allowance 0/0) 6.Terms of payment, their function and criteria of selection. Forms of payment. (allowance 0/0) 7.Bills of exchange and cheques in international trade. Promissory notes, bills of exchange, their essentials, negotiation and discounting. Guaranteed bills of exchange. Essentials of cheques, types of cheques. (allowance 0/0) 8.Documentary forms of payment: Documentary letters of credit, definition, types, applicability. Documentary collection, definition, types, applicability. Open account trade, clean payment, forwarder's collection on delivery, system of electronic banking. (allowance 0/0) 9.Intermediaries in international trade, seller-buyer relations, sole distributor/importer contracts, international agency contracts, commercial representation, consignment sale. (allowance 0/0) 10.Logistics in international trade, shipping operations, contractual arrangements in shipping operations, forwarder's contract, shipping contract, charters. International multimodal transport , documents used in international transport of goods, optimizition of transporting systems. General Average. (allowance 0/0) 11.Registration of internal EU business, reporting of value added taxes, VIES system, Intrastat reporting system, registration of special customs arrangements as re-exports, consignment stocks etc. (allowance 0/0) 12.Insurance and underwriting in international trade. Insurance of international transport, New Marine Policy. Insurance of international credits and documentary instruments. Insurance of product liability. Insurance of fairs and exhibitions. (allowance 0/0) 13.Risk of non payment, definition, methods of reduction. Deferred letters of credit, bills of exchange, bank guarantees, insurance of credits, retention of title to the goods. Debt management, hedging. (allowance 0/0)
learning_outcomes: - realize business transactions in the intra-trade including differences to trading with third countries outside the EU -analyse business operation tools in the above mentioned area.
assessment_methods: Term paper: 20 % Final test: 80 %
literature: Basic: Sato, A. et al. (2021). Mezinárodní obchodní operace (1st ed.). Nakladatelství Oeconomica. Recommended: Machková, H., Sato, A., & Zamykalová, M. (2002). Mezinárodní obchod a marketing : [praktická výkladová encyklopedie]. Grada.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of the course is that students get to know basic institutes of commercial law in the Czech Republic and also have an overview of their position in the system of Czech law. The effects of the legal regulations on practical issues of doing business will be explained with the help of case studies.
learning_outcomes: -define basic terms of business law -determine, which of the legal enactments should be applied in particular issues of business activity and to define their interrelations, -understand principles of enforcing law in business disputes, -characterize basic types of contracts and their essential elements, -asses validity and effect of contractual arrangements, -define consequences resulting from breaching obligations arising from contractual relations.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Final oral exam: 94 %

Praha: Wolters Kluwer, a.s., 2015. ISBN 978-80-7478-735-5. Platné právní předpisy určené vyučujícím RABAN, P. Občanské právo hmotné - závazkové právo. Brno: Václav Klemm, 2014. ISBN 978-80-87713-11-2

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: Students will learn the purpose of a business plan, its users, methods, techniques and principles of preparing a business plan. They will learn what are the criteria for the evaluation of business plans from the perspective of banks from the perspective of the investor. They will be acquainted with the possibilities of financing business plans. Part of the course are also presenting the principles of proper business plan.
course_content: 1.Business plan (allowance 6/0) a. purpose b. users c. structure 2.The processing of business plan (allowance 6/0) a. the principles of processing b. the procedure of processing c. mistakes and pitfalls of processing 3.Criteria for evaluation of business plans (allowance 5/0) a. general and specific criteria b. plan drawn up for bank c. plan drawn up for the investor 4.Financing of business plans (allowance 4/0) a. possibilities b. benefits of each option c. risk of each option 5.Presentation of business plan (allowance 5/0) a. principles of good presentation b. Presentation of business projects
learning_outcomes: - define the process of creating a business plan, - prepare a situational analysis, - design an appropriate business model, - prepare a business plan, - present a business plan, - suggest criteria for the evaluation of the business plan from the perspective of the firm's owner, - assess business plans from the perspective of the bank, - assess the business plans from the investor's perspective, - compare the possible ways of financing a business plan.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 20 % Term paper: 70 % Presentation or defence: 10 %

BUZAN, Barry; BUZAN, Tony. Myšlenkové mapy : probuďte svou kreativitu, zlepšete svou paměť, změňte svůj život. Brno: Computer Press, 2011. ISBN 978-80-251-2910-4.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The course “Corporate finance 1” is focused on acquisition of needful foundation of corporate finance which is need for orientation in corporate finance and for financial decision making. Therefore in advance students must master time value of money and its application it into practice. Then for understanding of corporate finance it is necessary also knowledge of financial statements. These problems are tightly connected with financial analysis. Its explication would be lead to basic practical habits financial statements analyzing.
course_content: 1. An essence of financial management, corporate objectives, rational and irrational behavior of investor. 2. Agency theory. The impact of agency costs on market value of firm. 3. Time value of money, interest rate (gross, net, nominal, real, effective, ...), interest compounding (discrete, continuous), present and future value of money, annuity (due and ordinary), perpetuity, loan with annuity payments. 4. Utilization of time value of money in investment decision making process into shares and bonds. 5. Final accounts and financial statements (balance sheet, income statement, cash flow statement). 6. Financial analysis, steps of financial analysts, indicators of returns (including pyramidal decomposition), cost level, assets and capital turnover, liquidity, leverage, on the basis of capital market data, prediction of financial distress.
learning_outcomes: - understand an essence of financial management, - master and apply time value of money technique and apply it to practice, - elaborate financial analysis.
assessment_methods: Final test: 100 %
literature: Basic: Marek, P. (2009). Studijní průvodce financemi podniku. Ekopress. Recommended: Radová, J., Dvořák, P., & Málek, J. (2013). Finanční matematika pro každého (8th ed.). Grada Publishing.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The course Corporate finance 2 is attended to master the problems, which is necessary for taking financial decisions as operating so also investment decisions. Teaching begins from managerial accounting, and continues through short-term financial management and long-term financial management, up to financial plan building. Course objective consists mainly in learning of practical procedure for financial decisions making. Co-part of course topic represents questions connected with financial distress management.
course_content: 1. Managerial accounting. Costing of costs and price. Budgeting. 2. Short-term financial management. Inventory, accounts receivables and cash management. Alternatives of short-term financial sources. 3. Long-term financial management. Investment decisions. Net present value, index of net present value, internal rate of return, payback period, etc. 4. Financial structure management. Theoretical approaches. Alternatives of long-term financial sources. Model to lease or to buy. 5. Financial planning: principles, coordination, methods and outputs. 6. Short-term financial plan, structure of cash inflow and outflow, balancing of cash surplus and cash shortage, cash pooling. Suggestion for financial distress management.
learning_outcomes: elaborate cost allocation and build cost budgets, orientate in inventory, receivables and cash management, master capital budgeting techniques and apply it to practice, choose appropriate long-term and short-term financial sources, discuss problems of optimal financial structure, build financial plan on the basis of available data, propose measures to corporate management in financial distress.
assessment_methods: Final test: 100 %
literature: Basic: Marek, P. (2009). Studijní průvodce financemi podniku. Ekopress. Recommended: Durčáková, J., Oceláková, P., & Valach, J. (2010). Investiční rozhodování a dlouhodobé financování. Ekopress. Fibírová, J., Král, B., Matyáš, O., Menšík, M., Petera, P., Wagner, J., Zralý, M., & Šoljaková, L. (2018). Manažerské účetnictví. Management Press.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: Course Corporate finance 3 is focused on selected problems in corporate financial management. First problem represents risk because every financial decision is connected with danger that our real results will be differ from expected results. Next problem consist in taxation. Therefore students would be orientated in tax system. As third problem students will be deal with income distribution and loss settlement. Following questions deal with business valuation and discernment between investment and market value. Final part of explication will be devoted to financial aspects of corporate integration and holding finance.
course_content: 1. Risk in financial management. Risk, its concept, types, measurements methods and its influence on corporate financial decision taking. 2. The impact of taxation on financial management. Tax system of Czech Republic. Basic terms of income tax and value added tax. 3. Income distribution and loss settlement. Dividend policy. Dividend types (preferred, common, stock, regular, special, etc.). 4. Investment versus market value. Methods of business valuation. 5. Financial aspects of corporate integration: forms, motives, methods against hostile takeover. Holding finance (planning, transfer pricing, etc.).
learning_outcomes: to understand an essence of risk and uncertainty, to master calculation of risk indicators and apply it to practice, orientate in tax system of Czech Republic, evaluate an impact of taxation on financial decisions making, to distinguish between various types of dividend and between various types of dividend policy, to discern between market and investment value, to master valuation techniques, to compare advantages and disadvantages of mergers and acquisitions, to analyze problems of holding finance.
assessment_methods: Term paper: 30 % Final test: 70 %
literature: Basic: Marek, P. (2009). Studijní průvodce financemi podniku. Ekopress. Recommended: MAREK, Petr. Rozdělování hospodářského výsledku a dividendová politika. Praha: Ekopress, 2000. ISBN 80-86119-28-9. Láchová, L., & Vančurová, A. (2018). Daňový systém ČR 2018. 1. VOX a.s. Mařík, M. (2018). Metody oceňování podniku : proces ocenění, základní metody a postupy. Ekopress.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of the course is to develop professional knowledge in an area of business management with emphasis on each phase of company growth and specifics of particular growth phases, strategy formulation and strategic management, performance, and change management, crises in business, and their possible solutions.
course_content: 1. Historical development of management (allotment 4/0) a. Classical period of management, human relations, contingency approaches c. Development of management in the 20th-century d. Current trends in management in the 21st century 2. Strategic management of the enterprise (allotment 5/0) a. Description of the most important managerial tools and their use b. Formulation of mission, vision, strategy. Determination of objectives c. Planning. Organizing. Decision making. Implementation d. New trends in strategy and planning 3. Business growth models (allotment 4/0) a. Greiner's model. Churchill and Lewis model b. Internal and external determinants of prosperity c. Performance and prosperity of the enterprise, possibilities of their increase 4. Organizing (allotment 4/0) a. Classical organizational structures. b. Organizational charts. Number of subordinates. c. Modern trends in organizing. 5. Change management in the company (allotment 6/0) a. Consequences of turbulent environments. Uncertainty, risk, change b. Innovation and its management. Supporting innovation c. Technology and change management d. Changing corporate culture. Diversity. Change in the nature of work and workplaces e. Selected change management models and their application f. Implementing change in the enterprise, managing resistance to change 6. Crisis management in the enterprise (allotment 3/0) a. Causes and prevention of crises b. Options for dealing with crisis situations c. Social responsibility and ethics. Dilemmas
learning_outcomes: - describe and explain historical stages in management development from the 19th century to the present - design a corporate mission and vision and link them to the organization's strategic, tactical and operational plans - identify the main factors in improving business performance and propose appropriate measures to strengthen it, design and implement basic management decisions at the various stages of business development - diagnose business dysfunction and propose corrective and restructuring measures, manage the business in an environment of unstable environment, continuous change and uncertainty - understand and manage relationships in a matrix, network, and virtual organization, delegate work effectively, promote self-management, self-control, agile management and effectively promote change.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 20 % Presentation or defence: 30 % Final test: 40 % Evaluation of another team's presentation: 10 %
literature: Basic: Daft, R. L. (2016). Organization theory and design. Cengage Learning. SCHERMERHORN, John R. Exploring management. Hoboken: John Wiley & Sons, 2012. ISBN 978-0-470-87821-7. Cejthamr, V., & Dědina, J. (2010). Management a organizační chování (2nd ed.). Grada Publishing.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of this course is to deepen the knowledge of students concerning labour law and its position within the system of Czech law. Students will also get to know to practical challenges in the area of labour relations and to the possibilities of finding a solution in managerial practice.
course_content: 1.Labor law in the legal system. Labor relations. (allowance 2/0) 2.Establishment and commencement of employment. (allowance 2/0) 3.Changes in employment. (allowance 2/0) 4.Working hours, rest periods, and vacation. Obstacles to work. (allowance 2/0) 5.Remuneration for work. (allowance 2/0) 6.Occupational health and safety. Employee care and special working conditions for certain employees. (allowance 2/0) 7.Termination of employment. Effective management of labor disputes (allowance 4/0) 8.Prohibition of discrimination and unequal treatment in labor relations (allowance 2/0) 9.Liability relationships (allowance 4/0) 10.Agreements on work performed outside of employment (allowance 2/0) 11.Introduction to collective labor law (allowance 2/0)
learning_outcomes: -manage personal agenda, -contract and cancel labour relations, -assess validity and effects of labour acts, -realise basic rights and duties of the employer from the point of view of the manager, -fulfil the duties of employers set in public law (especially labour codex), -negotiate with employee representatives, e.g. within collective bargaining.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 12 % Final oral exam: 88 %
literature: Basic: KOLDINSKÁ, Kristina; POLÁK, Petr; PRESSEROVÁ, Petra; TOMŠEJ, Jakub. Antidiskriminační zákon a související předpisy. Praha: Wolters Kluwer, 2023. ISBN 978-80-7676-615-0. HŮRKA, Petr. Pracovní právo v bodech s příklady. Praha: Wolters Kluwer, 2022. ISBN 978-80-7552-637-3. Recommended: PICHRT, Jan. Pracovní právo. 2. vydání. Academia iuris. V Praze: C.H. Beck, 2025. ISBN 978-80-7699-024-1 platné právní předpisy určené vyučujícím

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: Students will get to know to the importance of trade, especially retail, in national and world economy and they will get familiar with business environment of retailing. The course enables the students understand transnational character and strategy of high-power firms in relationship to their suppliers, competition and customers. Issues of retail typology and principles of fair entrepreneurship are covered in this course as well. Students will also learn how to apply some methods of analysis and management used in retail business.
course_content: 1.Importance, definition and functions of business in the economy, importance of retailing, business environment, development. (allowance 2/0) 2.Retailing -- importance for the GDP creation, retailing as the inter-link between production and consumption, development of business performance. (allowance 2/0) 3.Wholesale trade and its forms according to different viewpoints, modes of supply, importance of ECR. (allowance 2/0) 4.4. Business concentration and its measurement, development of retailing from the point of view of different chains, internationalization, diversification, globalization (allowance 2/0) 5.Process of the integration of retail firms, strategy of the expansion on foreign market, forms of market entry. (allowance 2/0) 6.Cooperation in retailing and its forms, decision making on a cooperation form. (allowance 2/0) 7.7. Store Retail -- formats of retailing from the point of view of systemic and instrumental characteristics, choice of level of the retail circle, Store Retail -- formats of retailing from the point of view of systemic and instrumental characteristics, choice of level of the retail circle 7. Store Retail -- formats of retailing from the point of view of systemic and instrumental characteristics, choice of level of the retail circle Store Retail -- formats of retailing from the point of view of systemic and instrumental characteristics, choice of level of the retail circle (allowance 2/0) 8.Nonstore Retail -- direct sale, mail-order trade, electronic trade -- development, strategy, application. (allowance 2/0) 9.Characteristics of retail operations, performance of a retail firm and its measurement, calculation of the number of necessary staffs, staff motivation. ( (allowance 2/0) 10.Analysis of a retail turnover from the point of view of its development as well as structure, measurement of the efficiency and profitability of a retail firm, assessment of the importance of a retail unit's localization as the impact on its performance. (allowance 3/0) 11.Territorial analysis in retailing, purchasing power of population, Buying Power Index of a locality, efficient range of the capacity of a retail unit for the given locality, stream buying, rate of realization etc. (allowance 4/0)
learning_outcomes: -be able to work with national and international statistics describing trade, -be able to use tools of performance analysis and trade management including HR management, -be able to use current terminology concerning retail formats, -know tendencies of development
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Term paper: 30 % Final test: 64 %
literature: Basic: Zeman, J. (2025). Retail business. VŠE, Nakladatelství Oeconomica. Recommended: CIMLER, Petr; ZADRAŽILOVÁ, Dana. Retail management. Praha: Management Press, 2007. ISBN 978-80-7261-167-6.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The course introduces modern concepts of business logistics. Methods of inventory management and inventory optimization, ways of calculating trade concentration and location analysis are taught. It also provides knowledge in the field of consumer protection.
course_content: 1.Business logistics, definition, functions. (allowance 0/0) 2.Logistical solutions of retail firms. (allowance 0/0) 3.Optimization of stores. Models of stores. (allowance 0/0) 4.Conditions of the territorial analysis, determination of basic concepts. (allowance 0/0) 5.Methods of the territorial analysis. (allowance 0/0) 6.Consumer protection -- theoretical basis, genesis. (allowance 0/0) 7.Consumer protection in the European Union and in the Czech Republic. (allowance 0/0)
learning_outcomes: - define aims of business logistics, - calculate inventory levels important for managing inventory, - Identify and apply the appropriate method for calculating the concentration , - explain the importance of location analysis, - understand the state consumer policy.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Final test: 40 % Final oral exam: 54 %
literature: Basic: CIMLER, Petr; ZADRAŽILOVÁ, Dana. Retail management. Praha: Management Press, 2007. ISBN 978-80-7261-167-6.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of this course is to teach the students use marketing tools in the area of business, especially in retail companies. Methods used when gaining information through market research are emphasised. Price and price survey, especially from the point of view of the consumer, are given attention when talking about marketing mix. With respect for the trends in communication, the issue of in-store marketing is stressed in the course.
course_content: 1. Consumer vs. customer, consumer behaviour, models of consumer behavior 2. Customer in this time and in future, his life style 3. marketing and psychology access to typology of consumers 4. Typology of customers according to research agency GfK, AISA, communications agency Young & Rubicam and TN Sofres 5. Market research as part of information system retailing company, sorts of research, source of informations. Respondents for typy market research, selections of respondents 6. Aplication of kvantitive market research in retailing company 7. Aplication of kvalitative market research in retailing company 8. Mark vs. Brand (substance, diferences, importance for retailing company strategic managing of brand. Aplication private labels of retailing chains 9. Marketing and commercial communications in retailing company, substance, form, characteristics, importance 10.Forms of ATL a BTL marketing communications activities (In-Store, Sales Promotion, Sampling, Merchandising, Couponing, Event Marketin g and their aplication in retailing company 11.CRM (Consumer Relationship Management)and its aplication in retailing company 12. Pricing promotion in retailing company, methods of pricing promotion
learning_outcomes: - use marketing tools in the area of business focused mainly on retail companies, - use particular methods of market research to gain information needed to use marketing tools, - prepare a survey of brand awareness using aided awareness and top of mind awareness methods, - prepare a price survey with the aim to find out the expected price, test the price scale and set a price threshold, - apply particular methods of qualitative market research - propose a marketing strategy for retail company or for its business operation unit.
assessment_methods: Term paper: 40 % Final test: 60 %
literature: Basic: BÁRTA, Vladimír; POSTLER, Milan; PÁTÍK, Ladislav. Retail marketing. Praha: Management Press, 2009. ISBN 978-80-7261-207-9. BÁRTA, Vladimír; POSTLER, Milan. Retail marketing : (přepracované vydání). Praha: Press21, 2020. ISBN 978-80-907529-1-7. JESENSKÝ, Daniel. Marketingová komunikace v místě prodeje : POP, POS, in-store, shopper marketing. Praha: Grada Publishing, 2020. ISBN 978-80-271-1724-6.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The course aims to develop specific communication skills of participants for interpersonal communication both with their co-workers and clients.
course_content: 1.Communication skills -- Johari windows, feedback, empathy and active listening, giving and accepting of feedback, interaction. (allowance 0/0) 2.Non-verbal communication -- mimicry, gesticulation, proxemics, haptics, paralinguistics, creation of personal image. (allowance 0/0) 3.Construction of efficient presentation. Differences of personal, telephonic and electronic communication. (allowance 0/0) 4.Assertiveness and communication -- role of assertive, submissive, aggressive and manipulative communication, assertive rights. (allowance 0/0) 5.Preparation and training of presentation by using video-presentation. (allowance 0/0) 6.Communication with different personality types, analysis of their motivation (allowance 0/0) 7.Communication with problematic persons. (allowance 0/0) 8.Causes of communication failures, noises, using efficient techniques to influence and manage conflict situations. Principles of transaction analysis. (allowance 0/0) 9.Emphasis on differentiation of motivation from manipulative techniques. Team roles suitable for individual personality types. (allowance 0/0)
learning_outcomes: - manage principles of successful communication, namely active listening, questioning - interpret elements of non-verbal communication - criticise constructively - prevent conflicts - communicate effectively with partners of different personality types - provide and accept feedback in a professional way - communicate assertively - defend against manipulative and aggressive conversation
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 78 % Term paper: 22 %
literature: Basic: DHAWAN, Erica. Digitální řeč těla : jak budovat důvěru a vztahy bez ohledu na vzdálenost. V Brně: CPress, 2023. ISBN 978-80-264-4687-3. DALE CARNEGIE TRAINING (FIRMA). Naslouchejte!. Praha: Dobrovský s.r.o., 2021. ISBN 978-80-277-0247-3. LOŠŤÁKOVÁ, Olga. Empatická a asertivní komunikace : jak zvládat obtížné komunikační situace. Praha: Grada, 2020. university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of the course is
course_content: 1.Research of the consumer behaviour at three levels: national economy, household as the basic economic unit, consumer as an individual. (allowance 0/0) 2.Trends in consumption, corridors, boom symptoms. (allowance 0/0) 3.Household, structure of household, lifecycle and impacts on consumption. (allowance 0/0) 4.Household equipment, reproduction, logistical curve, innovation. (allowance 0/0) 5.Segmentation: tree, behavioural and psycho-graphical. (allowance 0/0) 6.Features and limits of the quantitative and the qualitative research, main features of the methodology. (allowance 0/0) 7.Psychological determinants of the consumer behaviour. (allowance 0/0) 8.Price, pricing dimension, dimension of the price perception by a consumer. (allowance 0/0) 9.Models of the decision making by a consumer and predication of its behaviour. (allowance 0/0)
assessment_methods: Term paper: 50 % Final test: 50 %

Praha: Grada Publishing, 2017. ISBN 978-80-271-0206-8. Recommended: Bárta, V., & Bártová, H. (2009). Homo spotřebitel. Oeconomica.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of the course is to equip the students with specialized knowledge of statistical processing and analysis of data which can be used in trade, including understanding the methodology. Students learn how to present data and interpret characteristics gained by using statistical methods.
course_content: 1.Basic statistical terms, types of variables, frequency tables and frequency charts. (allowance 0/0) 2.Summary characteristics: measures of location (averages, percentiles, mode), measures of variability. (allowance 0/0) 3.Evaluation of trends in time series: fixed base indices, chain index numbers, growth coefficient, chronological average, moving averages. (allowance 0/0) 4.Presentation of data and analysis results by means of tables and charts. (allowance 0/0) 5.Comparing indicators by means of indices and differences: elementary index numbers, summary index numbers, consumer price index, inflation rate. (allowance 0/0) 6.Principles of investigation of relationships between statistical variables: correlation analysis, analysis of variance, regression analysis. (allowance 0/0) 7.Analysis of data obtained on the basis of questionnaire survey (by means of contingency tables). (allowance 0/0) 8.Population estimation: point estimators, interval estimation. (allowance 0/0) 9.Principles of hypotheses testing: one-sample and two-sample tests, pair test, goodness of fit test. (allowance 0/0) 10.Tests of independency: chi-squared test in the contingency table, F test in analysis of variance, t test in correlation analysis. (allowance 0/0)
learning_outcomes: - apply and interpret summary statistical characteristics, - describe trends in time series, compare indicators by means of indices and differences, - describe principles of investigation of relationships between statistical variables and to apply them on specific cases, - analyze data obtained on the basis of questionnaire survey, - apply statistical hypothesis testing.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 6 % Term paper: 20 % Final test: 74 %
literature: Basic: Řezanková, H., & Löster, T. (2013). Základy statistiky (1st ed.). Oeconomica. Recommended: Langhamrová, J., Löster, T., & Řezanková, H. (2009). Statistické metody a demografie. Vysoká škola ekonomie a managementu. Löster, T., & Řezanková, H. (2009). Úvod do statistiky. Oeconomica.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: Prepare students to perform audits in the era of the 4th Industrial Revolution. To highlight the consultative approach of the audit profession, suggest possible audit focus and share current methods for handling audit engagements. Explain audit procedures, audit documentation, data analytics, and professional communication of audit results. During the course, teams solve case studies reflecting the entire audit process in an international context.
course_content: 1. Risk Management (Economical Context, Risk Theory, Risk Management, Business Management) 2. Business Control (Control Environment, Internal Control, External Control, Control vs. Audit) 3. Audit (Audit History, Profession Development, Audit Types, Legal Bases) 4. External Audit (Audit Theory, Audit Objectives, ISA International Standards, Chamber of Auditors) 5. Internal Audit (Audit Theory, Audit Objectives, IPPF International Standards, International Institute of Internal Auditors) 6. Auditor (Modern Concept of Audit Services, Skills, Education, Certification) 7. Audit Planning (Risk Analysis, Law, Management Requirements, Audit Plan) 8. Audit Process (Preparation, Audit, Completion, Discussion) 9. Audit Techniques and Data Analytics (Interview, Observation, Analytics, Statistics) 10. Audit Documentation (Audit Trail, Audit Program, Audit Records, Audit Report) 11. Audit Trends (Big Data, ESG, ICT, Industry 4.0) 12. Audit Communication (Communication Theory, Audit Communication, Audit Meetings, Dress Code)
learning_outcomes: - define audit and its tasks in the modern corporation strategy; - design an analysis of potential risks and select processes for audit; - prepare an audit programme containing current topics (Big Data, ESG, ICT, Industry 4.0); - plan audits, select samples, design methods, and verify the risk processes of the enterprise; - formulate recommendations for management, and report on audit results.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation 50 %: 50 % Final test 50 %: 50 % Total 100 %: 100 %
literature: Basic: Anthony, R. N., Govindarajan, V., Hartmann, F. G. H., Kraus, K., & Nilsson, G. (2021). Management control systems. McGraw-Hill. Crawford, L., Gray, I., & Manson, S. (2015). Audit process : principles, practice and cases. Cengage Learning. MURDOCK, Hernan. Auditor essentials : 100 concepts, tools, and techniques for success. Boca Raton: CRC Press, Taylor & Francis Group, 2019. ISBN 978-1-138-03691-8. Recommended: Arens, A. A., Beasley, M. S., Elder, R. J., & Hogan, C. E. (2020). Audit analytics : data science for the accounting profession. Cham: Springer, 2020. ISBN 978-3-030-49090-4.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The course is focused on basic and advanced methods used in financial theory. The aim is to make the students familiar with sequences and series, principles of time value of money and basic principles of financial markets.
course_content: 1. Sequences, boundedness and monotonicity, limit of a sequence. 2. Series, convergence of series, geometric series, power series, summing up series, absolute convergence. Testing convergence. 3. Time value of money. Simple, compound and continuous interest. Frequency of compouding. Nominal, effective and real interest rates. 4. Annuity-immediate, anuity-due and continuous annuity. Present and future values of annuities and present value of perpetuities-immediate and due. 5. Net present value, internal rate of return, investment project appraisal using annuities and perpetuities. 6. Securities, perpetuities. Bonds, yield. Valuation of stock - dividend discount model. 7.Basics of stochastical finance.
learning_outcomes: solve the following tasks 1) use sequences in solving mathematical problems, 2) work with series, calculate their sum and analyse their convergence, 3) use various kinds of compounding, determine the effective and real interest rates, 4) calculate the present and future value of annuity with various frequencies of payments and compounding, 5) valuate bonds and stocks, work with exchange rates, 6) analyse the risk and return of a portfolio.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation 10 %: 10 % Mid-term test(s) 30 %: 30 % Final test 60 %: 60 % Total 100 %: 100 %
literature: Basic: Jackson, T. M., & Parkinson, R. (1989). Financial mathematics and statistics. Checkmate Gold. LUENBERGER, D. G. Investment Science. Oxford: Oxford Univerzity Press, 1997. ISBN 01951008094.

aims: Course is oriented on getting knowledge in using the office application software MS Office 2013. university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The course provides fundaments of Logistic management. The course contains overview about essential logistic strategies, services, processes such as procurement, supply planning and management, warehousing, distribution planning and transportation planning and organization including technical bases of transport. Students acquire orientation in logistic strategies, concepts and in particular processes.
course_content: - Introduction to Logistics and logistic strategies - Supply chain management - Logistic services - Procurement - Means of transport in logistics - Loading units in logistics - Demand planning, supply planning and management fundamentals - Logistics in distribution - Warehousing
learning_outcomes: Course graduates are familiar with logistic services, strategies, technologies and processes in modern supply chains. In addition to that, they are equipped with knowledge and skills in specific areas of logistic system. Furthermore, identification of imperfections and defects in logistic system and defining viable solutions of them are skills that students gain during the course. Last but not least, students experience work in teams during logistic classes. Gained knowledge and skills are equal to logistic disponents and in selected areas provide basic skills equal to logistic managers.

Partnership in logistics. Praha: Radix, 2000. ISBN 80-86031-24-1.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: Macroeconomics provides a basic theoretical background for understanding how the economy operates. It provides students information about the main macroeconomic measures, money market, the macroeconomics equilibrium theory, the open economy problems, inflation, unemployment, economic growth, business cycle and macroeconomics politics.
course_content: 1.Introduction into macroeconomics. 2.Macroeconomics aggregates: gross domestic product, gross national income. 3.Aggregate expenditures and equilibrium product. 4.Money and money market. 5.Aggregate demand and aggregate supply. 6.Economic growth and business cycle. 7.Unemployment. 8.Inflation. 9.Exchange rate. 10.External economic equilibrium: balance of payments, external debt. 11.Monetary policy. 12.Fiscal policy. 13.International trade and trade policy.
learning_outcomes: use the main macroeconomic categories, indicators, and models. These subjects provide students with a theoretical background for the further study of the following economic disciplines. Upon successful completion of this course, students will be able to: -understand the basic principles of the macroeconomic environment affecting the decisions of economic subjects (households, companies) -search and make a basic analysis of main macroeconomic data -describe the main impacts of inflation, unemployment, the foreign exchange rate on economic subjects (households, companies) -understand the current fiscal and monetary policies, including an assessment of their potential impacts on the economy (individual economic subjects - households, companies) - apply the main theoretical approaches to solve questions from economic reality which are connected with the changing macroeconomic environment
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation 2 %: 2 % Term paper 8 %: 8 % Mid-term test(s) 40 %: 40 % Final oral exam 50 %: 50 % Total 100 %: 100 %

J., Miles, D., & Scott, A. J. (2012). Macroeconomics : understanding the global economy. John Wiley & Sons. Shiller,B. The Macro Economy Today, 12th edition, 2009. ISBN 978-0077247409.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The objective of the course is to introduce students to the topic of a general management applicable for every type of organisation - profit and non profit, SME's, big and multinational. Successful management requires knowlwedge of basic managerial functions and key managerial approaches and techniques.
course_content: Introduction to management. Managerial functions - planning, deciding, organising, motivation, leadership, control. Communication, HR management, information and knowledge in management, identity of organisation. Profile of manager, ethics in management and managerial styles. Challenges of management in globalised world. Development of management.
learning_outcomes: explain present-day approaches in management, they will be able to understand differences between various managerial functions and master another know-how connected with management: - identify and recognize current and possible future trends in development of management science - define major roles of managers of different levels in organisations - critically evaluate ethical problems and solve them - construct organisational structures for concrete organisations - use different managerial tools and methods - recognise different managerial styles and approaches Specifically: They will understand the role of managers in organisations and importance of management for organisations. They will be able to articulate and use planning categories. They will learn to choose proper methods and tools that will help them to decide and will be able to suggest proper organisational structure depending on concrete situation of organisation. They will be able to identify adequate tools of motivation and execute controlling processes in proper way. In addition they will be able to choose and apply proper style of leadership and motivation, decide on right style of management. Great attention is paid to their ability to identify and estimate ethical problems and dilemmas and to solve them. The course is finished with excursion to the history of management that allows students to synthetise the knowledge of the course. Overall learning outcomes:
assessment_methods: Mid-term test(s) 40 %: 40 % Final test 60 %: 60 % Total 100 %: 100 %
