(2017). Základy soukromého práva v příkladech. Vydavatelství a nakladatelství Aleš Čeněk, s.r.o. Moravec, T. et al. (2020). Základy práva pro neprávníky po rekodifikaci soukromého práva (5th ed.). Vydavatelství a nakladatelství Aleš Čeněk, s.r.o. Recommended: Občanský zákoník, Zákon o obchodncíh korporacích, Zákon o živnostenském podnikání, Občanský soudní řád

course_title: Regulation and Supervision of Financial Market
university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The course provides the student with a comprehensive view of risk management of the financial system at the macroeconomic and microeconomic level with an emphasis on bank regulation and supervision. It focuses on financial stability issues and the application of macroprudential policy tools to limit the materialization of systemic risk in the financial sector. The course focuses on theoretical aspects of regulatory practices and models of institutional arrangements for financial market regulation and supervision. It explains in detail the relationship between a bank's key economic risks and the basic regulatory requirements for bank capital, liquidity and funding. The course also examines the exercise of supervision and the review and evaluation process combining prevention and sanction mechanisms. Based on the concept of corporate governance, the role of the internal control system and its application is explained using the example of bank regulation and supervision. The objective of statutory audit as an assurance function in relation to the financial statements and the annual report is explained to the students. The basic principles of audit regulation are also discussed, focusing on both global regulation and regulation within the EU and therefore the Czech Republic. The difference between statutory audit and other types of audit, especially forensic audit, is also explained. Last but not least, the student is introduced to the concept of a sustainability report and its verification by an auditor.
course_content: 1. Financial market institutional arrangements and the nature and evolution of regulatory rules 2. Institutional arrangements for regulation and supervision in Europe 3. Macro-prudential and micro-prudential approaches to regulation and their relationship to financial stability 4. Capital adequacy and capital requirements 5. Financial risk management and regulation (credit risk) 6. Financial risk management and regulation (market risk) 7. Financial risk management and regulation (liquidity and funding risk) 8. Supervisory performance and the review and evaluation process 9. Corporate governance, building internal controls and linkage to financial sector regulation 10. Internal control system and its relationship to internal and external audit 11. Audit of financial statements, audit objectives, audit procedures and audit specifics for public interest entities 12. Forensic investigation, forensic investigation techniques and specifics of forensic investigation in the financial sector.
learning_outcomes: - understand the institutional arrangements for regulation and supervision at national and supranational level - understand the structure of powers and responsibilities of supervisory institutions at macro and micro level - assess the links between the key institutions of regulation and supervision within the EU - explain approaches to financial stability analysis and be able to identify the main sources of financial instability - explain the nature of the financial cycle and discuss financial cycle indicators - understand the objectives and instruments of macro-prudential policy - understand the nature of the capital adequacy requirement, understand the application of capital buffers and be able to relate leverage and the leverage ratio - understand the issues involved in determining capital requirements for the relevant types of risk - identify the impact of regulatory practices on banks' economic decision-making - understand supervisory methods and understand the sources of remote supervision - understand the principles of corporate governance and the relationship of internal control systems to financial market regulation and supervision - define the basic principles of a functional internal control system and its relationship to internal and external audit - understand the importance of statutory audit (audit of financial statements and annual report)
assessment_methods: Mid-term test(s): 40 % Final test: 60 %
literature: Basic: BLAHOVÁ, Naděžda. Rizika bank a jejich regulace. Jesenice: Ekopress, 2018. ISBN 978-80-87865-47-7. PFEIFER, Lukáš. Finanční stabilita bankovního sektoru a možnosti jejího prosazování. Plzeň: Nava, 2020. ISBN 978-80-7211-585-3. KRÁLÍČEK, Vladimír; MOLÍN, Jan. Vnější a vnitřní kontrola z pohledu managementu. Praha: Wolters Kluwer, 2014. ISBN 978-80-7478-557-3. Hindls, R., Hronová, S., Králíček, V., Kříž, P., Müllerová, L., & Šindelář, M. (2017). Auditing. Oeconomica, nakladatelství VŠE. Recommended: ČNB - Zpráva o finanční stabilitě (různá vydání)

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of the first part of this classwork is to get students acquainted with elements of probability, elementary statistical conceptions and possibilities of data analyzing and prezenting as well as with some elements of deductive and inductive ways of thinking. The aim of the second part is to get students acquainted with most used statistical procedures and methods, with conditions for their use, advantages and shortcomings and to teach students correctly interpret results.
course_content: - analysis of one-dimensional statistical data - operations with random events and their probabilities - random variables and their probability distributions - the most important types of probability distributions - inferences based on random samples - methods of measurement of dependence among quantitative and qualitative variables, further some procedures used for categorical data analysis, review of classificatory approaches and some non-parametric methods, respectively - methods of time series analysis - methods of analysis of sample data - methods of statistical comparison.
learning_outcomes: select suitable statistical method for the description and analysis of given economic problem and to interpret the results obtained with the use of statistical method applied including the interpretation of computer outputs.
assessment_methods: Mid-term test(s): 40 % Final test: 60 %
literature: Basic: HINDLS, Richard; ARLTOVÁ, Markéta; HRONOVÁ, Stanislava; MALÁ, Ivana; MAREK, Luboš; PECÁKOVÁ, Iva; ŘEZANKOVÁ, Hana. Statistika v ekonomii. 1. vyd. Příbram: Professional Publishing, 2018. 395 p. ISBN 978-80-88260-09-7. Marek, L. (2013). Statistika v příkladech. Professional Publishing. Recommended: Weiss, N. A. (2016). Elementary statistics. Pearson. Garcia, T. (2016). Student's solutions manual Weiss: Elementary statistics, ninth edition. Pearson. Benson, G., McClave, J. T., & Sincich, T. (2022). Statistics for business and economics. Pearson.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: Accounting as an information tool for the decision-making of an entity’s owners, managers, investors in capital markets, creditors and other users of financial statements. Fundamental elements of financial statements and their content. Impacts of transactions and events on financial position and performance. European and worldwide regulation and harmonisation of accounting.
course_content: 1. Introduction to accounting: the nature, principles of financial accounting, characteristics of accounting. Objectives of financial accounting, scope of financial reporting, users of financial statements. 2. Accounting categories and definitions, characteristics of accounting elements, accounting cycle, standardization of financial reporting. Elements of accounting method: balance sheet equation, nature of accounts, double-entry and duality concept.Trial balance. 3. Documentation, controls, measurement; transaction analysis. Accounting cycle. Income statement accounts: expenditures and receipts, expense and revenue, classification of expenses by nature and by function. 4. Business models: a trade company vs. a manufacturer. 5. Conceptual framework. Features of world harmonization process. EU Directives, EU, US GAAP and IFRS. 6. Non-current assets: Definition, classification, measurement, depreciation and amortisation. 7. Inventory: definition, classification, measurement. 8. Investments: definition, classification, measurement, presentation, transactions. 9. Receivables and revenues: recognition principle, measurement, accruals and contingent assets. 10. Liabilities and provisions: classification, measurement, accruals and deferrals, contingent liabilities. 11. Equity: classification, features of different types of companies, capital contribution and distribution, Statements of Changes in Equity, Statement of Comprehensive Income. 12. Cash flow statement. Notes to financial statements. 13. Audit. Issuing of financial statements. Annual Report.
learning_outcomes: - understand the content of financial statements for external users; - define and explain the nature of fundamental elements of financial statements; - record the basic economic transactions on the face of financial statements; - master the accounting cycle, including double-entry; - prepare an elementary set of financial statements; - describe the main features of accounting regulation and harmonisation both in national and international context.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 15 % Mid-term test(s): 15 % Final test: 60 % Case Studies: 10 %
literature: Basic: Dvořáková, D., Černý, V., Zelenková, M., Svoboda, M., Skálová, J., Roubíčková, J., Procházka, D., Pelák, J., Bokšová, J., & Vašek, L. (2020). Účetnictví I. : sbírka příkladů a úloh k procvičení. Vysoká škola ekonomická v Praze, nakladatelství Oeconomica. Pelák, J. (2009). Účetnictví v příkladech : repetitorium k základům účetnictví. Oeconomica. Kovanicová, D. (2005). Finanční účetnictví : světový koncept IFRS/IAS. Polygon. Janhuba, M. (2010). Teorie účetnictví : (výběr z problematiky). Oeconomica. ROUBÍČKOVÁ, Jaroslava. Závěrečné testy z Účetnictví I. Praha: Oeconomica, nakladatelství VŠE, 2017. ISBN 978-80-245-2222-7. Recommended: KOVANICOVÁ, Dana. Jak porozumět světovým, evropským, českým účetním výkazům. Praha: Polygon, 2004. ISBN 80-7273-095-9. Mezinárodní standardy účetního výkaznictví (český překlad originálu standardů IFRS 2005). Praha : Institut svazu účetních, 2006. Dvořáková, D. (2010). Základy účetnictví (1st ed.). Wolters Kluwer. KOVANICOVÁ, Dana. Abeceda účetních znalostí pro každého. Praha: Trizonia, 1993.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The aim of the course is to provide students with a strong linkage between the theory of public finance (and in the broader concept of public economy theory as well) and modern public finance practice in order to improve their adaptability in the labor market. Another aim is to extend the theoretical, methodological and analytical knowledge and skills of students about the basic elements of the public budget system. During the lectures, various approaches are presented at the theoretical level. Public finance measures (including tax measures), individual expenditure programs and projects are analyzed and discussed. In the seminars the students will learn how to work with the most important internet data sources, how to use the basic tools of quantitative analysis and to prepare the project in groups. The whole course of 1VF200 is focused on the public finances of the Czech Republic, at the same time in the context of Czech Republic membership in the European Union. Upon successful completion of this course, students will be able to work with the most important data sources available on the Internet, understand the public finance system in the Czech Republic, use modern methods of public expenditure management, evaluate the efficiency of public expenditure programs and analyze direct and indirect impacts of tax measures using basic quantitative methods.
course_content: Fundamental theoretical background of public finance - state interventions in the economy; critical view of neoclassical approach towards allocation function of public finance - Coase, Buchanan; elementary problems of efficiency of state economic interventions. Seminar: Basic information sources about public finance - Ministry of Finance, state budget, the analysis of expenditure elasticity of public expenditure of the most important budget chapters. Public finance and problems of public choice - theory of public choice; public choice as a tool of a decision-making support in public finance; budgetary process and political system in the Czech Republic; median voter. Seminar: Basic information sources about public finance - system ARIS/Monitor, testing the hypothesis of election cycle on the municipal level. Redistribution, social justice and public budgets - definition of terms; social justice; redistribution; measures of redistribution rate - Gini coefficient and other indicators; tools of public finance for implementation of redistribution politics; impacts of redistribution on economic efficiency. Seminar: Calculations of Gini and Hoover indicators on model data, construction of Lorenz curve. Public revenues - elementary types of public revenues; trends in development of public revenues in the Czech Republic; tax revenues and their macroeconomic value in the Czech Republic; tax expenditures, efficiency of Czech tax system, Eco-Tax reform. Seminar: Basic steps in microsimulation, the analysis of the impact of expenditures and taxes on income distribution in society. Impacts of tax and benefit system on labour market - tax and benefit system in the Czech Republic, Make Work Pay indicators, the analysis of model households, microsimulation. Seminar: Basic steps in microsimulation, calculation of selected Make Work Pay indicators (METR (EP), NRR). Audit and control in the public sector - the methods of evaluation of public expenditure efficiency, control systems of public budgets in the Czech Republic, 3E methodology. Seminar: Analysis of option of application of 3E methodology on special case prepared by SAI. RIA and CBA - modern methods for assessing impacts of government interventions, application of the CBA concept, problem of time preferences. Seminar: PC simulation in the field of time preferences - impacts on the efficiency of public expenditure projects. Public procurement and PPP projects I- public tender procurement as a tool for rising effectiveness of public sector, strengths and weaknesses, problems of practical implementation in the Czech Republic. Seminar: Main data sources about public procurement (ISVZ, TED), PC simulation of different decision methods, testing the hypothesis of “Law of huge number”. Public procurement and PPP projects II- concession and quasi-concession, legislative framework in EU, ex-ante evaluation of efficiency. Seminar: Creation of a financial model of PPP and calculation of PSC. Public sector deficit in the Czech Republic - trends in development, monitoring, financing; public debt in the Czech Republic; development of the balance of public budgets in the Czech Republic; structural deficit; cyclical component of the deficit; long-term fiscal imbalance in the Czech Republic; reporting methodology . Seminar: Work with data of MF CR and CZSO about economy of public budgets, relationships between GFS and ESA 95. Public finance in the Czech Republic and the European Union - tendency in public budgets in the Czech Republic and requirements of the European Union, excessive deficit procedure; Convergence programmes and their assessment; reform proposals towards the Stability and Growth Pact and their potential impact on the Czech Republic’s position. Seminar: Analysis of Eurostat data about economy of public budgets in EU member countries, estimation of potential output, calculation of structural balance. The course includes an independent preparation and presentation of a seminar paper - a case study, e.g. in the field of empirical studies on the validity of selected economic and fiscal hypotheses, in the field of the system of public budgets, the budgetary process of the state budget, the financial management on the municipal level, including the budgetary determination of taxes or local fees, the review of the management of the selected municipality or financial control in public administration.
learning_outcomes: - understand the system of public finance in the Czech Republic; - work with key data sources on public finances, which are available on the internet (MF CR, OECD, Eurostat); - create and work with a simple microsimulation model using IT; - calculate the value of Make Work Pay indicators; - apply CBA to selected public expenditure programs; - calculate the output gap and divide public deficit to structural and cyclical components.
assessment_methods: Term paper: 10 % Mid-term test(s): 20 % Final test: 70 %
literature: Basic: Hillman, A. L. (2003). Public finance and public policy : responsibilities and limitations of government. Cambridge University Press. Brown, C. V., & Jackson, P. M. (2003). Ekonomie veřejného sektoru. Eurolex Bohemia. Recommended: Ochrana, F. (2003). Veřejná volba a řízení veřejných výdajů. Ekopress. PAVEL, Jan. Ekonomické aspekty veřejných zakázek. Praha: Oeconomica, 2007. ISBN 978-80-245-1260-0. Hamerníková, B., & Maaytová, A. et al. (2007). Veřejné finance (3rd ed.). ASPI. Medveď, J. (2005). Verejné financie. Sprint.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The subject is focused on macroeconomic topics, together with the connection with economic policy and the acquisition of conceptual apparatus.
course_content: 1. Introduction to macroeconomics - subject of research, macroeconomic aggregates. Domestic product, aggregate expenditures and pensions, savings from GDP, public sector, foreign trade 2. Consumption, investment and equilibrium GDP 3. Money and demand for cash balances, money as a means of exchange, nominal and real money balances, demand for cash balances 4. Banking system, money creation and money market, money supply and its changes, money market equilibrium 5. Aggregate demand, aggregate supply and potential product, "shocks" from demand and supply 6. Economic cycle, financial crisis, banking panic 7. Economic growth, natural resources, accumulation of capital, new technologies, market size, social system 8. Inflation - measurement, redistribution, demand, cost, inflation expectations, inertia inflation, Phillips curve, natural rate of unemployment, accelerating inflation 9. Exchange rate, interest rate parity, purchasing power parity theory (absolute, relative), exchange rate intervention, stable exchange rate regimes 10. Balance of payments and external debt, balance of payments, balance of payments, balance of goods and services, an imbalance of goods and services, foreign debt 11. Public budgets and taxes, redistribution, grey economy, Laffer Curve, budget deficits and sovereign debt, budget deficit financing, political cycle, displacement effect 12. Monetary policy, adaptive and rational expectations, monetary policy inefficiency, monetary targeting, inflation targeting, disinflationary policy 13. Modelling in economics
learning_outcomes: - enumerate the main terms and concepts used in the core macroeconomic courses and to explain their meaning, - demonstrate the range of issues and topics that fall within the macroeconomic field and connect them to economic reality, - define basic macroeconomic variables and explain their importance in the real economy, - recognize trends in the economy as a whole, - illustrate and explain potential changes in the real economy (macroeconomic changes) using the AS-AD model, - link the theoretical knowledge with the development of the real economy, e.g. apply theory to explain changes in macroeconomic variables, - summarize the basic knowledge of macroeconomics as a science discipline and explain their use, e.g. for economic policy purposes, - explain the main differences of economic schools and demonstrate these differences in the application of economic policy, - apply the theoretical knowledge in making its own recommendations for economic policy and explain the possible impacts of specific types of economic policy.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation 30 %: 0 % Final test 70 %: 100 % Total 100 %: 100 %
literature: Recommended: Additional notes/handouts will be provided by the course instructor. BLINDER, Alan S. Macroeconomics: Principles and Policy. 14th ed. Boston, MA: Cengage Learning, 2021. ISBN 978-0-357-12793-0. LEVI, Maurice D. International Finance. New York: Routledge, 2019. ISBN 978-0-367-18142-0. MANKIW, N. Gregory. Principles of Macroeconomics. Boston, MA: Cengage Learning, 2021. ISBN 978-0-357-13496-9.

university: Prague University of Economics and Business
aims: The subject acquaints students with the concept of marketing as a modern concept of organization and activity management. It clarifies the importance and position of marketing in organizational structures, content and tools of modern marketing. Students will learn the concepts of marketing, including their mutual relations and links, will be able to define the essence of marketing, the concept and importance of creating and implementing a quality marketing concept for the management needs of each organization. They will get acquainted with the internal and external environments influencing the functioning of each organization, the marketing concept of the market and its breakdown, the marketing information system, the behaviour of consumers, the basic parts of the marketing mix and the importance of the strategic marketing management of the organization in the conditions of the developed market economy.
course_content: 1. The role of marketing in the company and in the enterprise 2. Market, consumer market analysis 3. Business marketing strategy 4. Marketing research and information system 5. Marketing mix and its set of tools for target markets 6. Product policy 7. Pricing policy 8. Distribution policy 9. Marketing communication 10. Marketing management 11. Services Marketing 12. International marketing 13. Green marketing in push/pull strategies
learning_outcomes: - link all the elements of the business environment to the marketing and create an adequate marketing mix, - modify the current marketing strategy of the subject while respecting the effects of the marketing environment, - identify market opportunities within the research environment, - use individual marketing mix tools (including their sub-mixes) to achieve the target state according to the marketing strategy, - link ethical issues to marketing activities within the application of marketing strategy, - apply individual marketing policies within the marketing mix application, - identify current trends in marketing, - explain the basic advantages of applying marketing management in the process of managing entities.
assessment_methods: Active lecture/seminar/workshop/tutorial participation: 10 % Term paper: 20 % Presentation or defence: 10 % Final test: 60 %
literature: Basic: Armstrong, G., & Kotler, P. (2021). Principles of marketing. Pearson. Marketing management. Boston: Pearson Education, 2016. ISBN 9781292092713.
